Details: Ozonation can effectively oxidize carbofuran based on the results of one bench-scale study.  Carbofuran removals of 71 to 95 percent were achieved and it was observed that the removal effectiveness can be increased by increasing the reaction temperature over the range of 10 to 40 degrees celsius. It should be noted that the pH for this experiment was 2, which is atypical for drinking water treatment.
